items.title: Court clears way for auction of Jamal Yunos’ seized assets after failure to pay Theresa Kok
items.summary: Lawyer Datuk SN Nair, who represented Kok, said the High Court today dismissed the interpleader claim application submitted by Jamal’s son, Muhammad Amin Daniel, who claimed that the seized items belonged to him. “Muhammad Amin Daniel’s application was dismissed with costs of RM8,000. Jamal paid the damages.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain forecast for KL, Labuan and 9 states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds involving the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Labuan as well as nine other states until 9pm today. It said in a statement that adverse weather is set to hit Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Sik and Baling); Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Mualim); and Terengganu (Dungun and Kemaman). Other peninsula states set to be affected are Selangor (Hulu Selangor and Gombak); Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan and Pekan); Negri Sembilan (Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau, Jempol and Tampin); and Johor (Batu Pahat and Pontian).

items.title: Anwar: Everyone must respect election rules, ethics during Johor, Negri Sembilan polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded all parties to respect campaign rules and ethics during the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections. Earlier, the EC set July 11 as polling day for the Johor election, with nomination day set for June 27. Negri Sembilan, meanwhile, will go to the polls on Aug 1 , with the EC setting July 18 as nomination day.

items.title: Fahmi: MCMC forwards complaints to social platforms without bias
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) forwards complaints related to social media content to the relevant platforms without bias, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. He said each social media platform has its own community guidelines, and MCMC's role as a statutory body is to bring public complaints to their attention. "For MCMC, it receives complaints and forwards them to the platforms without bias, without fear or favour, and without being influenced by any party.

items.title: Muhyiddin's ex-aide rejects Pas accusation of greed in seat negotiations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former aide to Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, Datuk Dr Marzuki Mohamad, has questioned Pas' claim that Bersatu was greedy in the allocation of electoral seats. Marzuki, who represented Bersatu in seat negotiations from the Pakatan Harapan 1.0 administration through to the Perikatan Nasional (PN) era, said seat distribution among component parties followed an established process based on specific formulas. "The remaining 50 per cent, involving overlapping claims among component parties, is then referred to the central seat negotiation committee for resolution," he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s strength lies in managing differences fairly, religion must promote peace
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Malaysia’s strength lies not in uniformity but in its ability to manage differences fairly and justly, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Anwar also urged religious leaders to continue using their moral authority and influence to promote peace and justice, saying their role has become increasingly important amid growing global conflicts and humanitarian crises. Anwar said such developments only underscored the growing importance of religious leaders in advocating reason, tolerance and moral responsibility in an increasingly uncertain world.

items.title: Farm Fresh MD buys into dip as shares hit nine-month low
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Farm Fresh Bhd's co-founder, group managing director and chief executive officer Loi Tuan Ee has increased his stake in the dairy company through open-market purchases. The share accumulation comes as Farm Fresh's stock trades near its lowest level in about nine months. Loi also holds an indirect interest of more than 37.3 per cent in Farm Fresh through investment vehicles Farmchoice Foods Sdn Bhd and Rainforest Capital Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Gold futures reverse losses to end higher
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gold futures on Bursa Malaysia Derivatives pared yesterday's losses to end higher on Friday, in line with firmer US COMEX performance, amid a weakening US dollar. At the close, the spot-month June 2026 contract increased to US$4,232.70 per troy ounce from US$4,091.50 per troy ounce at Thursday's close; July 2026 rose to US$4,249.30 per troy ounce from US$4,106.50, and August 2026 increased to US$4,265.70 per troy ounce from US$4,124.20. The September 2026 contract rose to US$4,270.20 per troy ounce from US$4,128.70 on Thursday, while the October 2026 contract grew to US$4,289.30 per troy ounce from US$4,147.80 per troy ounce previously.

items.title: MAG group CEO Captain Nasaruddin appointed to IATA board of governors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) president and group chief executive officer Captain Nasaruddin A. Bakar has been appointed to the board of governors (BoG) of the International Air Transport Association (IATA). MAG and Malaysia Airlines Bhd non-executive director Datuk Amirul Feisal Wan Zahir said Nasaruddin's appointment is a testament to his deep expertise, leadership and long-standing contributions to the aviation industry. It noted that Nasaruddin will serve a three-year term until 2029, joining global industry leaders in providing strategic oversight and policy direction for IATA.

items.title: Trump says G7 support 'irrelevant', claims 'victory' in Iran war
items.summary: ISTANBUL: US President Donald Trump has dismissed any support from the G7 as "irrelevant" and claimed that "we have won the war in Iran" in a brief phone interview broadcast by Italian channel La7 on Friday, Anadolu Ajansi reported. Asked whether he had a message for G7 leaders, Trump said the US had not needed external support. It was a bit irrelevant, irrelevant!

items.title: Sultan Nazrin urges youth not to let faith diminish the dignity of others
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah has urged young people not to allow anyone to convince them that the dignity of their faith depends on diminishing the dignity of others. Delivering his royal address at the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders 2026 today, His Royal Highness said diversity was part of divine wisdom and should inspire people to compete in doing good. His Royal Highness said the future of faith and coexistence among religious traditions would not be secured by one generation alone.

items.title: Selangor strengthens sustainability agenda through RM2bln WTE investment
items.summary: KUALA SELANGOR: The Selangor government, through its subsidiary Worldwide Holdings Bhd (Worldwide), has invested approximately RM2 billion to develop three waste-to-energy (WTE) plants as part of a long-term plan to improve sustainable solid waste management in the state. "When WTE 2 is completed and fully operational, waste management capacity in Jeram will increase to approximately 3,500 tonnes per day. The remaining 2,000 tonnes will be managed through WTE 3 in Tanjung Dua Belas.

items.title: Pasir Gudang chemical leak: Scheduled waste facility owner, supervisor detained
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG: The owner and supervisor of a scheduled waste facility operating in an industrial area here were detained today after one of its tanks leaked scheduled waste chemicals that caused yellowish-orange smoke to fill the air yesterday. The Johor Department of Environment detained the 34-year-old plant owner and a 41-year-old supervisor for alleged breaches of environmental laws. State DoE director Dr Mohd Famey Yusoff said the arrests were carried out after a thorough investigation into public complaints over the incident, with enforcement teams confirming serious breaches of environmental laws, including failure to comply with scheduled waste management procedures and licence conditions under the Environmental Quality Act 1974.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia issues UMA query to Paragon Union after share price falls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd has issued an Unusual Market Activity (UMA) query to Paragon Union Bhd following a sharp fall in the company's share price today. In a Bursa filing, the exchange advised investors to take note of the company's response to the UMA query, which will be posted on Bursa Malaysia's website under company announcements. Bursa Malaysia said it requested Paragon Union to determine whether there were any undisclosed corporate developments, including those under negotiation or discussion, that could have contributed to the sharp movement in its share price.

items.title: Attractive valuations drive buying, Bursa Malaysia ends higher
items.summary: Among the heavyweight counters, Malayan Banking Bhd added six sen to RM10.72, Public Bank Bhd was five sen to RM4.83, Tenaga Nasional Bhd gained 12 sen to RM14.42, CIMB Group Holdings Bhd was nine sen better at RM7.39, and IHH Healthcare Bhd climbed 11 sen to RM8.78. Among the active stocks, Zetrix AI Bhd was flat at 81 sen, Top Glove Corp Bhd was 2.5 sen lower at 80.5 sen, AirAsia X Bhd added six sen to RM1.20, Capital A Bhd was unchanged at 40 sen, and Dagang NeXchange Bhd perked up 1.5 sen to 36 sen. Of the top gainers, Nestle (M) Bhd put on RM1.10 sen to RM95.60, Fraser & Neave Holdings garnered 94 sen to RM26.74, Ajinomoto (Malaysia) Bhd improved 58 sen to RM15.30, and Petronas Dagangan Bhd rose 48 sen to RM18.20. Among the top losers, Paragon Union Bhd plunged RM1.39 to RM3.27, Batu Kawan Bhd tumbled 96 sen to RM19.84, and Petronas Chemicals Bhd slid 46 sen to RM5.34, while Unisem M Bhd and Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd slipped 18 sen each to RM4.60 and RM20.76, respectively.

items.title: Religious leaders and youth: Advancing coexistence and social harmony
items.summary: The following is the full text of Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah's special address at the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders 2026 at the Kuala Lumpur Convention Centre on Friday. I want to speak about the people inheriting this world, young people, and about the difference between simply declaring peace and doing the hard work of building it. Young people today are asking fundamental questions: Where do I belong?

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar says religious leaders key in addressing moral crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the role of religious leaders had become increasingly important in upholding humanitarian values amid a deepening global moral crisis. "The role of religious leaders remains more relevant than ever because they represent the voice of conscience, reason, patience and tolerance — qualities that are in short supply in today's world," he said at the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders 2026 today. Also present were Muslim World League Secretary-General Sheikh Dr Mohammad Al-Issa, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Datuk Dr Zulkifli Hasan, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir, and Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.

items.title: Onn Hafiz urges BN machinery to campaign with discipline and decorum
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has urged the coalition's machinery to campaign with discipline and decorum ahead of the 16th Johor state election on July 11. "I call on the entire BN Johor machinery to campaign with full order and civility," he said. "Evaluate based on track record, stability, achievements and the ability to continue bringing Johor forward," he said.

items.title: Woman injured in off-duty accident eligible for Perkeso's Lindung 2/7 scheme
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) has confirmed that a woman who sustained serious injuries, including a fractured shoulder and leg, in an accident after a hiking activity at Bukit Chanang near Keningau, is eligible to receive benefits under the Lindung 24/7 (Lindung 24 Jam) scheme. In a statement today, Perkeso said Brissty Vidalyn Felicianus, 33, who is an active contributor under the scheme, was involved in an accident on June 2 when the four-wheel-drive vehicle she was travelling in veered into a ravine while descending from the hiking starting point. Perkeso said Brissty has been issued a medical leave certificate until June 15 and is scheduled to undergo surgery to insert metal implants to treat her fractures.

items.title: Auction set for seized Jamal assets in MP Teresa Kok costs dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The 14 household items seized from the home of Sungai Besar Umno chief Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos will be auctioned on June 22 to recover RM66,061.82 in unpaid legal costs owed to Seputeh member of parliament (MP) Teresa Kok. Sankara said one claimant, Mohd Razali Kamin, who said he owned several seized items, filed a notice of discontinuance and was ordered to pay RM2,000 in costs. "The interpleader claim by Muhammad Amin Daniel Jamal (Jamal's son) was dismissed with costs of RM8,000.

items.title: 1.88 million Malaysians benefit from mySalam; RM1.42 billion paid out
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Some 1.88 million Malaysians have benefited from the mySalam health protection scheme, with claims amounting to RM1.42 billion disbursed between 2019 and Dec 31 last year. "For hospitalisation benefits, 1.294 million recipients received a total of RM394.87 million. "Meanwhile, 89,085 recipients benefited from the critical illness coverage, with payouts totalling RM712 million, while 13,128 recipients received RM65.257 million for medical devices," he said at his weekly post-cabinet meeting press briefing.

items.title: Sultan Nazrin warns AI disinformation fuelling youth division
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, has warned that artificial intelligence (AI)-generated disinformation is fuelling division among youths at a scale and speed that traditional religious institutions are struggling to match. Delivering a royal address at the 3rd International Summit of Religious Leaders 2026 today, His Royal Highness said extremist narratives were reaching young people through digital platforms, often by exploiting grievances and religious language. Present were Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Muslim World League Secretary-General Sheikh Dr Mohammad Al-Issa, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir and Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.

items.title: Mesiniaga wins RM43.28mil contract from Maybank unit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mesiniaga Bhd has secured a RM43.28 million contract from Maybank Shared Services Sdn Bhd for the renewal of a licence subscription agreement over a three-year period ending May 31, 2029. This is the second contract Mesiniaga has secured from Maybank Shared Services this year. In April, the company won a RM51.64 million contract for cloud infrastructure software subscriptions and support services.

items.title: 36 questioned over Jaslinda's 14-day disappearance on Gunung Batu Putih
items.summary: IPOH: Police have recorded statements from 36 individuals regarding the disappearance of climber Jaslinda Saludin, who was found safe last Saturday, 14 days after she went missing on Gunung Batu Putih near Tapah. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said those who gave statements included Jaslinda, her husband, Haszman Othman, 61, and friends involved in the climbing activity. Last Saturday, Jaslinda, 49, was found safe by residents of Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu after going missing on May 24 while climbing Gunung Batu Putih.

items.title: Gold steadies, but heads for second straight weekly loss
items.summary: Spot gold was little changed at US$4,217.95 per ounce, as of 0843 GMT, but was down more than two per cent for the week. US gold futures rose three per cent to US$4,238.50. ANZ lowered its year-end price target for gold by US$400 to US$5,200 to reflect recent price volatility.

items.title: Fahmi disappointed as Lee Chean Chung quits PKR council, says voters deserve answers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today expressed disappointment over the resignation of Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung from the party’s central leadership council, saying that he owed an explanation to his voters and party members. Fahmi said he had known Lee for many years and believed the MP should have used the party’s internal platforms to raise any concerns rather than leaving the party. He said differing views within PKR were not uncommon and pointed to senior party leader Elizabeth Wong, who is also the former member of Selangor State Executive Council as an example of someone who regularly expressed views that differed from the party leadership while continuing to engage through party channels.

items.title: MIPP hails 'Reset' convention as platform for national renewal
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) has described the "Reset" convention tomorrow as an important platform for constructive discussions on national renewal, governance, economic resilience and Malaysia's future. While extending his congratulations and best wishes to Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin on the inaugural convention, MIPP president P. Punithan said the convention provided a timely opportunity for stakeholders to exchange ideas and deliberate on issues affecting the nation's development and future direction. "The 'Reset' Convention is an important platform for constructive discussions on national renewal, governance, economic resilience and Malaysia's future.

items.title: NGO seeks public help to sustain schools for mentally handicapped students
items.summary: The centres, run by the Selangor and Federal Territory Association for the Mentally Handicapped (SAMH) in Klang and Kuala Lumpur, are deeply ingrained in their daily routines, and keeping them operating costs RM150,000 a month. "This will affect us badly because without public donations, the coming months and years will be very difficult for us," said SAMH organising chairperson and deputy president, Tan Sri Hasmah Abdullah. The 62nd Anniversary Charity Dinner, SAMH's primary fundraising event and financial lifeline, is scheduled for June 20 at Dorsett Grand Subang, with the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, attending as the royal guest of honour.

items.title: Wisma Harapan lifeline at risk as funds run dry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: For 62 years, the Selangor and Federal Territory Association for the Mentally Handicapped (SAMH) has provided life-changing education and vocational training through its Wisma Harapan schools in Klang and Kuala Lumpur. The centre supports students with autism, Down's syndrome, ADHD and dyspraxia, with programmes ranging from early childhood intervention to adult vocational placements. Dila Hassan said her 34-year-old son, Muhammad Azim Hamzah, who has autism, expresses his deep attachment to the school in his own unique way.

items.title: PM directs ministries to gather input on foreign workers manpower
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed the relevant ministries to obtain detailed input from industries and sectors on foreign manpower requirements. Unity government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said this followed applications from several sectors seeking foreign labour, including the restaurant and construction industries. "The cabinet meeting today discussed applications from several sectors for foreign labour.

items.title: Hospitalisation, critical illness and medical device aid among RM1.42b MySalam payouts to Malaysians, says minister
items.summary: According to figures presented to the Cabinet, he said over 1.294 million recipients have received hospitalisation benefits amounting to RM394.87 million. “For critical illness claims, 89,085 recipients received payments amounting to RM712.68 million. For medical device assistance, 13,128 recipients received support worth RM65.257 million,” he added.

items.title: RM600,000 needed to demolish illegal Rohingya settlement in Sungai Tekali
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: RM600,000 is needed to demolish a four-storey illegal structure allegedly built by the Rohingya community in Sungai Tekali, Hulu Langat, Selangor. He said most members of the Rohingya community had already left the area, with only three families believed to remain. He described issues involving the Rohingya community as highly complex and challenging.

items.title: Amirudin says Selangor will act against all illegal settlements without considering nationality
items.summary: KUALA SELANGOR, June 12 — The Selangor government will take action against all illegal settlements in the state, including demolition, regardless of the occupants’ nationality, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said. He said enforcement measures are not based on sentiment or nationality but on land administration requirements, land-use conditions and compliance with local authority regulations. However, he said the state government must adhere to legal procedures, including giving landowners sufficient time to rectify breaches before stricter measures such as land forfeiture are imposed.

items.title: Fahmi: Putrajaya to collect detailed workforce data before deciding on foreign worker quotas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the government will gather more detailed workforce data before making any decisions on foreign worker quotas in the restaurant and construction sectors. He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had directed several ministries to engage with industry players and obtain a clearer picture of labour requirements. “As Malaysia currently maintains a policy on limiting the overall number of foreign workers, the prime minister directed the Human Resources Ministry, the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, the Housing and Local Government Ministry, and the Works Ministry to obtain more detailed information from industry players regarding labour requirements, including demand for foreign workers,” he said.

items.title: Mi Technovation executive director Heng Kok Lin retires
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mi Technovation Bhd has announced the retirement of executive director Heng Kok Lin effective today. Heng, 63, is a Singaporean national who has served on the group's board as an executive director, according to the company's filing with Bursa Malaysia. The company said Heng has no family relationship with any director or major shareholder of MiT and no conflict of interest with the group.

items.title: Fahmi says Lee should have raised concerns within PKR
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has expressed disappointment over Petaling Jaya member of parliament Lee Chean Chung's failure to use the party's internal channels to voice his views and concerns on the issues he raised. Fahmi, who is also communications minister, said Lee should have used party platforms to express his views and concerns. Fahmi said Lee had not attended Central Leadership Council meetings for quite some time.

items.title: PKR Youth says opposition blaming Anwar over internal turmoil is political distraction
items.summary: IPOH, June 12 — Angkatan Muda Keadilan (AMK) has described attempts to link Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to turmoil within the Opposition as a desperate effort to conceal the bloc’s leadership weaknesses. AMK chief Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim said it was unreasonable to blame Anwar for every internal crisis affecting the Opposition, and that the issues actually stemmed from power struggles and disputes over seat allocations. “Marzuki (Datuk Marzuki Mohamad) himself admitted that internal turmoil exists within the Opposition due to seat disputes and differing political interests.

items.title: Malaysians spend 15.3pc more in April to RM174.8b, but goods bought rise just 6.2pc, DOSM reports
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 12 — Malaysians spent more in April as wholesale and retail trade sales rose 15.3 per cent year-on-year to RM174.8 billion, outpacing a 6.2 per cent increase in the volume of goods sold, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin said wholesale trade recorded sales of RM83.5 billion in April, an increase of 24.1 per cent and a volume growth of 7.3 per cent, compared with the same month last year. He said retail trade registered sales of RM71.0 billion, an increase of 6.3 per cent or RM4.2 billion year-on-year.

items.title: Perak police chief: Complaints against Rohingya should be channelled through authorities
items.summary: IPOH, June 12 — The public is advised not to take action against the Rohingya ethnic group themselves but rather channel any complaints or relevant information to the police for appropriate action. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said any misconduct by the Rohingya ethnic group should be reported to the authorities and action will be taken in accordance with the existing law. “We will always conduct regular monitoring of the Rohingya ethnic group,” he said.

items.title: Selangor freezes ‘trans hiking’ and ‘hiking compress’ permits after string on recent incidents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — The Selangor State Forestry Department has suspended all applications for “trans hiking” and “hiking compress” activities across the state’s Permanent Forest Reserves, effective July 1. The suspension targets high-risk, trail-based activities, particularly multi-day or cross-country treks. The suspension specifically applies to “trans hiking” and “hiking compress” itineraries, activities that typically involve traversing remote sections of forest reserves, overnight stays, and limited emergency accessibility.

items.title: Unilateral child conversion laws in six states stand as High Court dismisses challenge by Indira Gandhi, 13 others
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — The High Court today dismissed a challenge by Ipoh mother M. Indira Gandhi and 13 others against state laws that allow children under age 18 to be converted to Islam without both parents’ consent. Court of Appeal judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman, who was a High Court judge when this case was heard, said that all 14 of them had failed to show they have locus standi, meaning the legal standing, to pursue this court challenge. But as this court challenge was filed as a public interest matter, the judge did not order Indira and the 13 others to pay for legal costs.

items.title: Two small parties, one big decision: Hadi puts Gerakan and MIPP at centre of Perikatan power play
items.summary: MARANG, June 12 — PAS will decide on Bersatu’s future in Perikatan Nasional (PN) at its central working committee meeting next Wednesday, party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed today, days after political cooperation between the coalition’s two biggest parties collapsed. Hadi said any expulsion from PN must be backed by a majority of the coalition’s four component parties: PAS, Bersatu, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP). Hadi said PAS would leave it to both parties to choose between PAS and Bersatu in charting the coalition’s direction.

items.title: Police arrest 12, including 10 Chinese nationals, in Melaka scam call centre bust
items.summary: MELAKA, June 12 — The police dismantled a ‘call centre’ syndicate suspected of being involved in a non-existent online investment scam with the arrest of 12 individuals in two raids at Taman Krubong Perdana here early on Wednesday morning. Acting Melaka Tengah district police chief Supt Halim Abas said that those detained comprised 10 male Chinese nationals, in addition to a local man and woman, all aged between 23 and 47 years. “In the first raid at 2.30 am, a team from the Melaka Commercial Crime Investigation Department (JSJK) arrested six individuals, consisting of five male Chinese nationals and one local man, all aged between 23 and 43 years.

items.title: Kedah schoolgirl unable to sit or stand since classmate’s chair-pulling prank in February
items.summary: PADANG BESAR, June 12 — A 12-year-old girl has allegedly been unable to attend school for nearly four months after sustaining injuries in a fall when the chair she was about to sit on was allegedly pulled away by a classmate during a lesson in February. Her mother, Norasikin Hasin, 32, claimed that the Year Six pupil suffered a soft tissue injury to her lower back and was subsequently diagnosed with functional neurological disorder and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) following the incident on Feb 11. She said the incident occurred during a teaching and learning session when her daughter attempted to sit down after greeting a teacher, but fell to the floor after her chair was allegedly pulled away by a female classmate.

items.title: Small portions, big problems — the incredible shrinking menu hits Malaysia
items.summary: According to the latest report from Retail Group Malaysia (RGM), sales at cafes and restaurants plummeted by 4.6 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 compared to the same period last year. This is a jarring reversal from the 3.1 per cent growth seen in the final quarter of 2025. Even more telling is the gap between hope and reality: as recently as March, F&B businesses had forecast a growth of 1.9 per cent for the quarter.

items.title: Obey Malaysian regulations or action will be taken, Anwar tells Rohingya refugees
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded Rohingya refugees in the country to comply with local laws and regulations at all times or face firm action. He said that although Malaysia adopts a humanitarian approach, the group remains subject to the country's legal system, including regulations governing the use of premises and the operation of businesses. “My advice to Myanmar nationals, particularly the Rohingya, is that they must also comply with the regulations in our country.

items.title: Why Johor votes first: Election Commission cites Negeri Sembilan’s surprise dissolution
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 12 — Logistical demands and preparations for election workers were among the main reasons Johor and Negeri Sembilan will not hold their state elections simultaneously, the Election Commission (EC) said today. “(The Negeri Sembilan assembly) was dissolved suddenly. “Negeri Sembilan only has the ballot boxes.

items.title: Labourer charged with robbing Razif Sidek at knifepoint in TTDI home, faces 14-year jail term and whipping
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — A Rohingya labourer pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to robbing national badminton legend Datuk Razif Sidek at knifepoint at the latter’s home last month. Mohamad Fawyas Yasin, 27, was accused of robbing Datuk Mohamad Razif Mohd Sidek, 64, at a house in Jalan Damansara, Taman Tun Dr Ismail, at about 12.20pm on May 19. Deputy public prosecutor J. Banusha objected to bail, citing the seriousness of the offence and the accused’s expired United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) card.

items.title: A fatal crash, a TikTok livestream and a ‘pistol’: How sharp-eyed viewers helped Kedah police arrest two men
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 12 — A TikTok livestream of a fatal crash took a dramatic turn yesterday when eagle-eyed viewers spotted what appeared to be a pistol inside the wreckage – a discovery that led police straight to two men now in custody. “Both suspects will be remanded this morning, and the case is being investigated under Section 36 of the Arms Act 1960, Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 and Section 44(1)(a) of the same Act,” he said. Section 44(1)(a) of the Road Transport Act 1987 governs driving under the influence of drugs or alcohol causing death.

items.title: TikToker remanded four days over online claims involving rescued hiker Jaslinda
items.summary: TAPAH, June 12 — A man in his 30s has been remanded for four days from today to assist investigations into a defamation case involving an Orang Asli village head (Tok Batin) of Kampung Lubuk Gaharu during a TikTok live stream. The case is being investigated under Section 500 of the Penal Code, Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act 1955. He was arrested at his home in Ipoh at about 1 pm yesterday following 11 police reports, Tapah district police chief Supt Abdul Malik Hasim said.

items.title: Malaysian embassy warns citizens to steer clear of Jakarta demonstrations
items.summary: JAKARTA, June 12 — The Embassy of Malaysia in Jakarta has advised Malaysians in Indonesia’s capital to avoid demonstration hotspots in Central Jakarta on Friday and follow updates from local authorities. In an advisory on Friday, the embassy said it was closely monitoring reports of demonstrations, particularly around Bundaran HI and the Arjuna Wiwaha Statue, also known as Patung Kuda, where traffic disruptions were expected throughout the day. The embassy said the protests were expected to cause traffic disruptions in several parts of Central Jakarta, particularly along Jalan MH Thamrin, Jalan Jenderal Sudirman and Jalan Medan Merdeka Barat.

items.title: Anwar to visit Sabah tomorrow for Madani Link launch and National Unity Week event
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to make a one-day visit to Sabah tomorrow to attend several events, including an official launch and community programmes. Upon completing the programme, he is scheduled to return to the state capital to attend the Sabah-level Melangkah Bersama Madani: Wanita Beriman, Wanita Bertahan event at the Sabah International Convention Centre at 2.30 pm. Anwar is scheduled to officiate the 2026 National Unity Week celebration at 4 pm.

items.title: EC sets Johor election on July 11, Negeri Sembilan on Aug 1
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 12 — The Election Commission (EC) today announced the polling dates for the upcoming state elections in Johor and Negeri Sembilan, setting the stage for a high-stakes political showdown in the southern region. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun confirmed that Johor voters will head to the polls on July 11, with nomination day scheduled for June 27 and early voting taking place on July 7. “For the 16th Negeri Sembilan elections, there will be 889,490 voters," the EC chairman added.

items.title: Ex-boyfriend held over rape allegation of 25-year-old woman, fatal stabbing of love rival in Perak
items.summary: Perak police chief Commissioner Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the suspect was detained at the Perlis Sentral Bus Terminal in Kangar at about 3.45pm yesterday following intelligence leads, The Star r eported today. Investigators believe the suspect had raped his ex-girlfriend, 25, after they met near a paddy field along Jalan Trans Perak here on June 9. A confrontation ensued after the 29-year-old allegedly rammed the suspect’s vehicle during a car chase along Jalan Trans Perak.

items.title: LHDN makes e-Derma mandatory for new tax exemption applications from June 15
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 12 — All new applications for approval under Subsection 44(6) of the Income Tax Act 1967 involving institutions or organisations (IOs) must be submitted through the e-Derma system from June 15, 2026. According to LHDN, the e-Derma system allows applications to be submitted online at any time without the need to visit an LHDN office or submit physical documents. However, new applications under Subsection 44(6) involving funds or Institutions/Organisations/Funds (IOTs) represented by tax representatives or tax agents must continue to be submitted manually together with supporting documents for the time being.

items.title: Early morning fire hits Gebeng waste plant, smoke visible across Kuantan (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 12 — Thick black smoke billowed across the Gebeng industrial area here after a fire broke out at a scheduled waste processing facility early today, prompting the temporary evacuation of about 200 students from a nearby training institute. National newswire Bernama reported Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim as saying that firefighters found the blaze had erupted inside the factory and involved oil-based materials and solvents. “The fire also affected the warehouse at the factory.

items.title: Miri main water pipeline repaired, supply to be return in stages within 12 hours
items.summary: MIRI, June 12 — Repair works on the damaged 1,100mm main pipeline at Mile 7 of Jalan Miri-Bintulu were completed at 8.30am, with water supply expected to be restored to affected areas in stages over the next 12 hours. Deputy Minister for Utility and Telecommunication Datuk Liwan Lagang said Sarawak Water is refilling the system, stabilising the distribution network, and carrying out flushing activities to ensure water quality is satisfactory before supply is fully restored to consumers. Among them were the Rural Water Supply Department, Fire and Rescue Department, Public Works Department, Sarawak Energy, Miri Resident’s Office, Miri District Office, Miri District Education Office, Miri City Council, Royal Malaysia Police, Malaysian Armed Forces, Malaysian Red Crescent, Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living, Miri Hospital, elected representatives, media personnel, and volunteers.

items.title: Umno sec-gen welcomes 21,858 new and returning members to ‘Rumah Bangsa’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Umno has recorded 21,858 new and returning members within less than two months since online registration was opened on April 20, party secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said today. He said the figure covers new members as well as former leaders and members who have rejoined the party nationwide, including some from Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu). “Since online registration was officially opened on April 20, 2026 until the Umno supreme council meeting on June 10, 2026, in less than two months, the number of new registrations and returning former leaders and members of Umno, including leaders from Bersatu across Malaysia, totalled 21,858,” he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Scam ‘salary’ of RM85 costs 37-year-old nearly RM100,000 in online job ruse
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 12 — A 37-year-old housewife lost RM95,715 after falling victim to an online job scam that promised easy earnings but delivered only a token RM85 commission, Harian Metro reported today. Kuala Terengganu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the victim was first drawn in after seeing a job advertisement on Facebook on May 8. Azli said the victim only received RM85 for the first two tasks before the scam escalated into larger transfers.

items.title: Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung quits PKR leadership, says party ‘no longer’ what it was
items.summary: Lee, however, said he will remain a PKR member and continue serving as an MP until Parliament is dissolved. “PKR today is no longer the PKR I once knew,” he said in a Facebook post today announcing his decision. Lee also said he would continue to carry out his parliamentary role, including supporting institutional reform bills expected to be tabled in June.
